problem: helix-cli has node_modules tracked in the Git index on active remote branches even though .gitignore lists node_modules/. This causes the helix-global-server implementation commit phase to fail because `git ls-files -m` reports node_modules as modified and `git add -A` refuses to stage ignored paths. The cleanup requires removing node_modules from the Git index and committing the removal on the branches Helix uses for tickets.

files.why: Already contains `node_modules/` as the first ignore rule (line 1). Also ignores `dist/`, `.env`, and `.env.*`. The ignore rule is present but insufficient when node_modules is already tracked in the index.

Defines the project as @projectxinnovation/helix-cli. Has no runtime dependencies (only devDependencies: @types/node and typescript). Scripts: build (tsc), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), test (tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js), prepare (npm run build).
TypeScript config: ES2022 target, Node16 module, strict mode. Compiles src/ to dist/.
CI workflow that publishes to npm on version tags. Runs npm ci and npm test. Validates tarball contents.

facts: .gitignore already contains `node_modules/` as the first line — the ignore rule is present.
node_modules directory does NOT exist in the current working tree.
The issue is that node_modules is tracked in the Git index on remote branches, so .gitignore alone does not prevent `git ls-files -m` from reporting it.
helix-cli has zero runtime dependencies (only devDependencies: @types/node and typescript).
The repo has a CI workflow (.github/workflows/publish.yml) for npm publishing.
Test infrastructure uses Node.js built-in test runner via `node --test dist/**/*.test.js`.
Two test files exist: src/lib/flags.test.ts and src/lib/resolve-ticket.test.ts.
The cleanup requires `git rm -r --cached node_modules` followed by a commit on each affected branch to remove node_modules from the index without deleting files from disk.
unknowns: Which specific remote branches currently have node_modules tracked — cannot verify without git commands (orchestrator-managed).
Whether the run branch (helix/fix/FIX-432-harden-ignored-path-staging-and-clean-helix-cli) was created from a branch that has node_modules tracked.
Whether there are additional Helix-relevant base branches beyond main and staging that need cleanup.
